subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloud vs. On-Premises Economics
description: A quick, practical guide to Cloud vs. On-Premises Economics.

hook.text: Dropbox spent $75 million on cloud. Then it built its own data centers and saved half. Was cloud a mistake?

buildup.text: At massive scale, owning hardware can beat cloud pricing. The breakeven point depends on utilization and growth.

discovery.text: Cloud wins for variable workloads. On-premises wins for steady, predictable, high-volume usage.

twist.text: Most companies aren't Dropbox-scale. For them, the operational burden of on-premises outweighs the savings.

climax.text: The answer isn't cloud or on-premises. Many companies use both — a hybrid approach that optimizes for cost.

punchline.text: Cloud or own? It depends on scale, skills, and workload.

quiz.question: When does owning hardware typically beat cloud pricing?
options: At very large scale with steady, predictable workloads
Always, since hardware is cheaper per hour than cloud
Only for companies with fewer than 10 employees
